basics.name: Md Toki Tahmid
basics.label: PhD Student in Computer Science
basics.summary: First-year PhD student at Princeton University working at the intersection of machine learning and computational biology, with a focus on protein function/regulation, RNA biology, genomics, and biological language models.

education.institution: Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology (BUET)

work.name: Department of Computer Science and Engineering, BUET
work.summary: Teaching Introduction to Machine Learning (CSE 472), Data Structures & Algorithms (CSE 106), and Signal Processing (CSE 220).

work.name: Computational Connectomics Lab, MIT
work.summary: Supervised by Nir Shavit and Lu Mi. Designed unified graph-based representations for artificial and biological neural networks; investigated structure-function correlations in ANNs and biological connectomes.
highlights: Structure Matters: Deciphering Neural Network's Properties from its Structure (NeurIPS 2024)

work.name: XuLab, Carnegie Mellon University
work.summary: Supervised by Min Xu. Developed positive-unlabeled learning methods for particle picking in cryo-ET datasets; designed algorithms for converting 2D bounding boxes into 3D bounding volumes.
highlights: TomoPicker: Annotation-Efficient Particle Picking in Cryo-Electron Tomograms (NeurIPS MLSB 2024)

work.name: Samsung Research and Development, Bangladesh
work.summary: Supervised by Dr. Anindya Iqbal. Fine-tuned LLMs for test case generation from Android applications; applied multi-modal LLMs to automate software testing.

work.name: CSE, BUET (Bayzid Lab)
work.summary: Supervised by Prof. Md. Shamsuzzoha Bayzid. Developed deep learning architectures for DNA-protein interaction prediction, proposed BiRNA-BERT for RNA language modeling, worked on hypergraph neural networks for protein-protein interaction.

work.name: Structural and Functional Genomics Research Group, CSE, BUET
work.summary: Supervised by Prof. M. Saifur Rahman. Applied LLMs for RNA structural prediction; developed efficient algorithms for biomolecular structural analysis using graph representation learning.

work.name: Next Generation Computing (NEC) Research Lab, CSE, BUET
work.summary: Supervised by Prof. A.B.M. Alim Al Islam. Built ML and simulation models for real-world challenges; analyzed HCI and BCI methods.

awards.title: Honda Y-E-S (Young Engineer and Scientist) Award
awards.summary: Recognized as one of four young researchers from Bangladesh for outstanding contributions to R&D. Awarded $3,000 grant and $10,000 for higher education.

awards.title: Spotlight Award — NeurIPS 2024 ENLSP Workshop
awards.summary: Spotlight award for BiRNA-BERT: Adaptive Tokenization for Efficient RNA Language Modeling.

awards.title: ACM SIGHPC Grant — HPC Immersion Program, SC23
awards.summary: Selected among 15 students worldwide to attend SC23 HPC Immersion Program in Denver, CO. Travel grant of $4,000 as a representative of underrepresented communities in HPC.

awards.title: RISE Research Grants (3×)
awards.awarder: Bangladesh University of Engineering and Technology
awards.summary: Three grants of $1,000 each for contributions in Computational Health & Biology (2024), ML for Health Informatics (2023), and ML for Scientific Discovery (2022).

awards.title: University Dean's Scholarship & Departmental Merit Scholarship
awards.summary: Dean's Scholarship for maintaining CGPA > 3.75 for four consecutive years; Departmental Merit Scholarship seven times for top standing in CSE.

awards.title: Best Student Poster Award
awards.summary: Awarded in 2021 ("Is It Really Dead?") and 2023 (BD-Vent: A Low-Cost Automated Mechanical Ventilator).

references.name: Prof. Min Xu
references.reference: Associate Professor, School of CS, Carnegie Mellon University — mxu1@cs.cmu.edu

references.name: Dr. Lu Mi
references.reference: Assistant Professor, Georgia Institute of Technology — lmi7@gatech.edu
